steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence opens with an appositive series—comma-separated noun phrases (“A coming-of-age story, a social meditation, and a sober gaze into the dark maw of French colonialism”). The blank must provide the subject for the verb interrogates.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
The rule is that the subject must match the appositive series's meaning.

The series describes a literary work, so the subject must refer to a literary work.

B correctly provides “Chraïbi's debut novel, published two years before Morocco gained its independence,”—a noun referring to a novel that aligns with these descriptions.

Options A, C, and D fail by providing subjects (Morocco or Chraïbi) that are not literary works.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
